def solve(m, n, waitCost)
  # Initialize a 2D array to store the minimum cost to reach each cell
  # We use a 2D array of size m x n, initialized to infinity
  # We also need to track the time step to determine whether it's a move or wait step
  # We'll use a 3D array: dp[time][i][j] = minimum cost to reach (i, j) at time 'time'
  # Since the maximum possible time is (m + n) steps, we can use a 2D array for each time step
  # However, since the time step is alternating between move and wait, we can optimize space
  # We'll use a 2D array for the current time step and a 2D array for the next time step
  # Initialize the starting cell (0, 0) with the entry cost
  dp = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n, Float::INFINITY) }
  dp[0][0] = (0 + 1) * (0 + 1) # Entry cost at (0, 0)
  
  # Directions: right (0, 1), down (1, 0)
  directions = [[0, 1], [1, 0]]
  
  # Time starts at 1 (since the first step is at time 1)
  time = 1
  
  while true
    # Create a new 2D array for the next time step
    next_dp = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n, Float::INFINITY) }
    
    # If we are at an odd time step (move step), we can move right or down
    if time % 2 == 1
      # Iterate over all cells
      directions.each do |dir|
        dx, dy = dir
        # Check if the new cell is within bounds
        if dx < m && dy < n
          # Calculate the entry cost of the new cell
          entry_cost = (dx + 1) * (dy + 1)
          # Update the next_dp with the minimum cost
          next_dp[dx][dy] = [next_dp[dx][dy], dp[dx][dy] + entry_cost].min
        end
      end
    else
      # If we are at an even time step (wait step), we need to wait in the same cell
      # So, we can only stay in the same cell and pay the wait cost
      # Iterate over all cells
      (0...m).each do |i|
        (0...n).each do |j|
          # If the current cell is reachable, add the wait cost
          if dp[i][j] < Float::INFINITY
            next_dp[i][j] = [next_dp[i][j], dp[i][j] + waitCost[i][j]].min
          end
        end
      end
    end
    
    # Check if we have reached the destination
    if next_dp[m-1][n-1] < Float::INFINITY
      return next_dp[m-1][n-1]
    end
    
    # Update dp to next_dp for the next time step
    dp = next_dp
    
    # Increment time
    time += 1
  end
end

    % Initialize a 2D array to store the minimum cost to reach each cell
    % We use a 2D array of size M x N, initialized to infinity
    % We also need to track the time step to determine whether it's a move or wait step
    % We'll use a 2D array for the current time step and a 2D array for the next time step

    Time = 1,

init_dp(M, N) ->
    lists:map(
        fun(_) ->
            lists:map(fun(_) -> infinity end, lists:seq(1, N))
        end,
        lists:seq(1, M)
    ).

set_entry_cost(DP, I, J, Cost) ->
    lists:map(
        fun(I1) ->
            lists:map(
                fun(J1) ->
                    if I1 == I andalso J1 == J -> Cost;
                       true -> value
                    end
                end,
                lists:seq(1, N)
            )
        end,
        lists:seq(1, M)
    ).

loop(DP, Directions, Time, WaitCost, M, N) ->
    NextDP = next_dp(DP, Directions, Time, WaitCost, M, N),
    case get_destination_cost(NextDP, M, N) of
        infinity -> loop(NextDP, Directions, Time + 1, WaitCost, M, N);
        Cost -> Cost
    end.

next_dp(DP, Directions, Time, WaitCost, M, N) ->
    NextDP = case Time rem 2 of
        1 ->
            % Odd time step: move step
            lists:map(
                fun(I) ->
                    lists:map(
                        fun(J) ->
                            lists:foldl(
                                fun({Dir, I1, J1}, Acc) ->
                                    if I1 == I andalso J1 == J ->
                                        EntryCost = (I1 + 1) * (J1 + 1),
                                        [Acc, DP[I1][J1] + EntryCost] ;
                                       true -> Acc
                                    end
                                end,
                                infinity,
                                Directions
                            )
                        end,
                        lists:seq(1, N),
                        lists:seq(1, M)
                    )
                end,
                lists:seq(1, M),
                lists:seq(1, N)
            );
        0 ->
            % Even time step: wait step
            lists:map(
                fun(I) ->
                    lists:map(
                        fun(J) ->
                            if DP[I][J] /= infinity ->
                                [DP[I][J] + WaitCost[I][J]];
                               true -> infinity
                            end
                        end,
                        lists:seq(1, N)
                    )
                end,
                lists:seq(1, M)
            )
    end,
    NextDP.

get_destination_cost(DP, M, N) ->
    DP[M-1][N-1].

def solve(m, n, waitCost)
  # Initialize a 2D array to store the minimum cost to reach each cell
  # We use a 2D array of size m x n, initialized to infinity
  # We also need to track the time step to determine whether it's a move or wait step
  # We'll use a 3D array: dp[time][i][j] = minimum cost to reach (i, j) at time 'time'
  # Since the maximum possible time is (m + n) steps, we can use a 2D array for each time step
  # However, since the time step is alternating between move and wait, we can optimize space
  # We'll use a 2D array for the current time step and a 2D array for the next time step
  # Initialize the starting cell (0, 0) with the entry cost
  dp = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n, Float::INFINITY) }
  dp[0][0] = (0 + 1) * (0 + 1) # Entry cost at (0, 0)
  
  # Directions: right (0, 1), down (1, 0)
  directions = [[0, 1], [1, 0]]
  
  # Time starts at 1 (since the first step is at time 1)
  time = 1
  
  while true
    # Create a new 2D array for the next time step
    next_dp = Array.new(m) { Array.new(n, Float::INFINITY) }

          entry_cost = (dx + 1) * (dy + 1)
          # Update the next_dp with the minimum cost

      # Iterate over all cells

          end
        end
      end
    end

    if next_dp[m-1][n-1] < Float::INFINITY
      return next_dp[m-1][n-1]
    end
    
    # Update dp to next_dp for the next time step
    dp = next_dp
    
    # Increment time
    time += 1
  end
end

solve(M, N, WaitCost) ->
    % Initialize a 2D array to store the minimum cost to reach each cell
    % We use a 2D array of size M x N, initialized to infinity
    % We also need to track the time step to determine whether it's a move or wait step
    % We'll use a 2D array for the current time step and a 2D array for the next time step
    % Initialize the starting cell (0, 0) with the entry cost

    InitialDP1 = set_value(0, 0, (0 + 1) * (0 + 1), InitialDP),

make_dp(M, N, Val) ->
    lists:map(fun _ -> lists:map(fun _ -> Val end, lists:seq(1, N)) end, lists:seq(1, M)).

set_value(I, J, Val, DP) ->
    lists:map(fun(I1) -> lists:map(fun(J1) -> if I1 == I andalso J1 == J -> Val; true -> DP[I1][J1] end end, lists:seq(1, N)) end, lists:seq(1, M)).

loop(M, N, WaitCost, DP, Time, _Acc) ->
    NextDP = next_dp(M, N, DP, Time, WaitCost),
    case get_value(M-1, N-1, NextDP) of
        infinity -> loop(M, N, WaitCost, NextDP, Time + 1, []);
        Cost -> Cost
    end.

next_dp(M, N, DP, Time, WaitCost) ->
    NextDP = make_dp(M, N, infinity),
    if
        Time rem 2 == 1 ->
            % Odd time step: move step, move right or down
            Directions = [[0, 1], [1, 0]],
            lists:foreach(fun({Dx, Dy}) ->
                I = Dx,
                J = Dy,
                if
                    I < M andalso J < N ->
                        EntryCost = (I + 1) * (J + 1),
                        NewCost = lists:nth(I, DP) ++ lists:nth(J, DP),
                        lists:map(fun(I1) -> lists:map(fun(J1) -> if I1 == I andalso J1 == J -> NewCost + EntryCost; true -> DP[I1][J1] end end, lists:seq(1, N)) end, lists:seq(1, M))
                end
            end, Directions);
        true ->
            % Even time step: wait step, wait in the same cell
            lists:foreach(fun(I) ->
                lists:foreach(fun(J) ->
                    if
                        DP[I][J] < infinity ->
                            NewCost = DP[I][J] + lists:nth(I, WaitCost) ++ lists:nth(J, WaitCost),

                    end
